title: Wolves Change Rivers Without Touching Them
script: Reintroduce a predator, and rivers literally move — without a single human hand. In 1995, wolves returned to Yellowstone after a 70-year absence. Elk stopped grazing riverbanks to avoid being hunted. Grass and trees exploded back. Roots locked in the soil. Rivers stopped eroding and carved new, stable channels — the geography of the park physically changed. But here's the wild part — the wolves never touched the rivers. Just their presence rerouted water. So next time someone says one species doesn't matter, send them this.
search_summary: Yellowstone wolf reintroduction 1995 triggered trophic cascade that altered river flow and landscape
comment_question: If removing one wolf changed a river, what did WE accidentally break? 🌊
